spec_match.rationale: A very thoughtful and correct parsing tool that extracts name and id from arbitrary HTML, maps them to fragment links, and flags case-insensitive matches (which break in Chrome 150 quirks mode) with custom fix suggestions.

interactive.rationale: Fully interactive. Users can type/paste their own HTML or click the preloaded examples ('matching', 'mismatch', 'mixed') to run a live audit in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb correctly describes how case-insensitive matching in quirks mode is checked, helping users audit their legacy sites.

edge_cases.rationale: Regex extractors are robust enough for standard anchors and tags, correctly approximating line numbers, distinguishing missing anchors from case-insensitive matches, and offering correct code fixes.

references.rationale: Correctly cross-references the sibling deep-link-tester, anchor-matching-demo, and quirks-vs-standards concepts.

design_system.rationale: Beautiful and clean monospace card styling, responsive boxes, colored verdict banners matching the platform showcase styles.

openQuestions.title: Handle anchor name encoding/special characters
openQuestions.detail: If anchor names contain URL encoding or special characters (like spaces or punctuation), regex checks may fail to match. Supporting proper URL-decoded/encoded matching would be a nice-to-have addition for legacy websites.

summary: An outstanding interactive audit tool that lets users paste their legacy HTML and immediately highlights any case mismatches that will break under Chrome 150's case-sensitive quirks mode rules.
